NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. With stringed instruments. A Contemplation of David. Give ear to my prayer, O God, And do not hide Yourself from my supplication.
NKJV.2: Attend to me, and hear me; I am restless in my complaint, and moan noisily,
NKJV.3: Because of the voice of the enemy, Because of the oppression of the wicked;  For they bring down trouble upon me, And in wrath they hate me.
NKJV.4: My heart is severely pained within me, And the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
NKJV.5: Fearfulness and trembling have come upon me, And horror has overwhelmed me.
NKJV.6: So I said, “Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest.
NKJV.7: Indeed, I would wander far off, And remain in the wilderness. Selah
NKJV.8: I would hasten my escape From the windy storm and tempest.”
NKJV.9: Destroy, O Lord, and divide their tongues, For I have seen violence and strife in the city.
NKJV.10: Day and night they go around it on its walls; Iniquity and trouble are also in the midst of it.
NKJV.11: Destruction is in its midst; Oppression and deceit do not depart from its streets.
NKJV.12: For it is not an enemy who reproaches me; Then I could bear it. Nor is it one who hates me who has exalted himself against me; Then I could hide from him.
NKJV.13: But it was you, a man my equal, My companion and my acquaintance.
NKJV.14: We took sweet counsel together, And walked to the house of God in the throng.
NKJV.15: Let death seize them; Let them go down alive into hell, For wickedness is in their dwellings and among them.
NKJV.16: As for me, I will call upon God, And the LORD shall save me.
NKJV.17: Evening and morning and at noon I will pray, and cry aloud, And He shall hear my voice.
NKJV.18: He has redeemed my soul in peace from the battle that was against me, For there were many against me.
NKJV.19: God will hear, and afflict them, Even He who abides from of old. Selah Because they do not change, Therefore they do not fear God.
NKJV.20: He has put forth his hands against those who were at peace with him; He has broken his covenant.
NKJV.21: The words of his mouth were smoother than butter, But war was in his heart; His words were softer than oil, Yet they were drawn swords.
NKJV.22: Cast your burden on the LORD, And He shall sustain you; He shall never permit the righteous to be moved.
NKJV.23: But You, O God, shall bring them down to the pit of destruction;  Bloodthirsty and deceitful men shall not live out half their days; But I will trust in You.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> on Neginoth<S>5058</S>, Maschil<S>4905</S>, A Psalm of David<S>1732</S>. Give ear<S>238</S> to my prayer<S>8605</S>, O God<S>430</S>; and hide<S>5956</S> not thyself from my supplication<S>8467</S>.

ESV.1: Give ear to my prayer, O God, and hide not yourself from my plea for mercy!
ESV.2: Attend to me, and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and I moan,
ESV.3: because of the noise of the enemy, because of the oppression of the wicked. For they drop trouble upon me, and in anger they bear a grudge against me.
ESV.4: My heart is in anguish within me; the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
ESV.5: Fear and trembling come upon me, and horror overwhelms me.
ESV.6: And I say, “Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest;
ESV.7: yes, I would wander far away; I would lodge in the wilderness; Selah
ESV.8: I would hurry to find a shelter from the raging wind and tempest.”
ESV.9: Destroy, O Lord, divide their tongues; for I see violence and strife in the city.
ESV.10: Day and night they go around it on its walls, and iniquity and trouble are within it;
ESV.11: ruin is in its midst; oppression and fraud do not depart from its marketplace.
ESV.12: For it is not an enemy who taunts me — then I could bear it; it is not an adversary who deals insolently with me — then I could hide from him.
ESV.13: But it is you, a man, my equal, my companion, my familiar friend.
ESV.14: We used to take sweet counsel together; within God's house we walked in the throng.
ESV.15: Let death steal over them; let them go down to Sheol alive; for evil is in their dwelling place and in their heart.
ESV.16: But I call to God, and the LORD will save me.
ESV.17: Evening and morning and at noon I utter my complaint and moan, and he hears my voice.
ESV.18: He redeems my soul in safety from the battle that I wage, for many are arrayed against me.
ESV.19: God will give ear and humble them, he who is enthroned from of old, Selah because they do not change and do not fear God.
ESV.20: My companion  stretched out his hand against his friends; he violated his covenant.
ESV.21: His speech was smooth as butter, yet war was in his heart; his words were softer than oil, yet they were drawn swords.
ESV.22: Cast your burden on the LORD, and he will sustain you; he will never permit the righteous to be moved.
ESV.23: But you, O God, will cast them down into the pit of destruction; men of blood and treachery shall not live out half their days. But I will trust in you.

NASB.1: For the choir director; on stringed instruments. A Maskil of David. Give ear to my prayer, O God; And do not hide Yourself from my supplication.
NASB.2: Give heed to me and answer me; I am restless in my complaint and am surely distracted,
NASB.3: Because of the voice of the enemy, Because of the pressure of the wicked; For they bring down trouble upon me And in anger they bear a grudge against me.
NASB.4: My heart is in anguish within me, And the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
NASB.5: Fear and trembling come upon me, And horror has overwhelmed me.
NASB.6: I said, 'Oh, that I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and be at rest.
NASB.7: 'Behold, I would wander far away, I would lodge in the wilderness. Selah.
NASB.8: 'I would hasten to my place of refuge From the stormy wind [and] tempest.'
NASB.9: Confuse, O Lord, divide their tongues, For I have seen violence and strife in the city.
NASB.10: Day and night they go around her upon her walls, And iniquity and mischief are in her midst.
NASB.11: Destruction is in her midst; Oppression and deceit do not depart from her streets.
NASB.12: For it is not an enemy who reproaches me, Then I could bear [it]; Nor is it one who hates me who has exalted himself against me, Then I could hide myself from him.
NASB.13: But it is you, a man my equal, My companion and my familiar friend;
NASB.14: We who had sweet fellowship together Walked in the house of God in the throng.
NASB.15: Let death come deceitfully upon them; Let them go down alive to Sheol, For evil is in their dwelling, in their midst.
NASB.16: As for me, I shall call upon God, And the LORD will save me.
NASB.17: Evening and morning and at noon, I will complain and murmur, And He will hear my voice.
NASB.18: He will redeem my soul in peace from the battle [which is] against me, For they are many [who strive] with me.
NASB.19: God will hear and answer them-- Even the one who sits enthroned from of old-- Selah. With whom there is no change, And who do not fear God.
NASB.20: He has put forth his hands against those who were at peace with him; He has violated his covenant.
NASB.21: His speech was smoother than butter, But his heart was war; His words were softer than oil, Yet they were drawn swords.
NASB.22: Cast your burden upon the LORD and He will sustain you; He will never allow the righteous to be shaken.
NASB.23: But You, O God, will bring them down to the pit of destruction; Men of bloodshed and deceit will not live out half their days. But I will trust in You.

NLT.1:
Listen to my prayer, O God.
Do not ignore my cry for help!
NLT.2:
Please listen and answer me,
for I am overwhelmed by my troubles.
NLT.3:
My enemies shout at me,
making loud and wicked threats.
They bring trouble on me
and angrily hunt me down.
NLT.4:
My heart pounds in my chest.
The terror of death assaults me.
NLT.5:
Fear and trembling overwhelm me,
and I can’t stop shaking.
NLT.6:
Oh, that I had wings like a dove;
then I would fly away and rest!
NLT.7:
I would fly far away
to the quiet of the wilderness. Interlude
NLT.8:
How quickly I would escape —
far from this wild storm of hatred.
NLT.9:
Confuse them, Lord, and frustrate their plans,
for I see violence and conflict in the city.
NLT.10:
Its walls are patrolled day and night against invaders,
but the real danger is wickedness within the city.
NLT.11:
Everything is falling apart;
threats and cheating are rampant in the streets.
NLT.12:
It is not an enemy who taunts me —
I could bear that.
It is not my foes who so arrogantly insult me —
I could have hidden from them.
NLT.13:
Instead, it is you — my equal,
my companion and close friend.
NLT.14:
What good fellowship we once enjoyed
as we walked together to the house of God.
NLT.15:
Let death stalk my enemies;
let the grave swallow them alive,
for evil makes its home within them.
NLT.16:
But I will call on God,
and the LORD will rescue me.
NLT.17:
Morning, noon, and night
I cry out in my distress,
and the LORD hears my voice.
NLT.18:
He ransoms me and keeps me safe
from the battle waged against me,
though many still oppose me.
NLT.19:
God, who has ruled forever,
will hear me and humble them. Interlude
For my enemies refuse to change their ways;
they do not fear God.
NLT.20:
As for my companion, he betrayed his friends;
he broke his promises.
NLT.21:
His words are as smooth as butter,
but in his heart is war.
His words are as soothing as lotion,
but underneath are daggers!
NLT.22:
Give your burdens to the LORD,
and he will take care of you.
He will not permit the godly to slip and fall.
NLT.23:
But you, O God, will send the wicked
down to the pit of destruction.
Murderers and liars will die young,
but I am trusting you to save me.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A contemplation by David. Listen to my prayer, God. Don’t hide yourself from my supplication.
WEB.2: Attend to me, and answer me. I am restless in my complaint, and moan,
WEB.3: Because of the voice of the enemy, Because of the oppression of the wicked. For they bring suffering on me. In anger they hold a grudge against me.
WEB.4: My heart is severely pained within me. The terrors of death have fallen on me.
WEB.5: Fearfulness and trembling have come on me. Horror has overwhelmed me.
WEB.6: I said, “Oh that I had wings like a dove! Then I would fly away, and be at rest.
WEB.7: Behold, then I would wander far off. I would lodge in the wilderness.” Selah.
WEB.8: “I would hurry to a shelter from the stormy wind and storm.”
WEB.9: Confuse them, Lord, and confound their language, for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
WEB.10: Day and night they prowl around on its walls. Malice and abuse are also within her.
WEB.11: Destructive forces are within her. Threats and lies don’t depart from her streets.
WEB.12: For it was not an enemy who insulted me, then I could have endured it. Neither was it he who hated me who raised himself up against me, then I would have hidden myself from him.
WEB.13: But it was you, a man like me, my companion, and my familiar friend.
WEB.14: We took sweet fellowship together. We walked in God’s house with company.
WEB.15: Let death come suddenly on them. Let them go down alive into Sheol.  For wickedness is among them, in their dwelling.
WEB.16: As for me, I will call on God. Yahweh will save me.
WEB.17: Evening, morning, and at noon, I will cry out in distress. He will hear my voice.
WEB.18: He has redeemed my soul in peace from the battle that was against me, although there are many who oppose me.
WEB.19: God, who is enthroned forever, will hear, and answer them. Selah. They never change, who don’t fear God.
WEB.20: He raises his hands against his friends. He has violated his covenant.
WEB.21: His mouth was smooth as butter, but his heart was war. His words were softer than oil, yet they were drawn swords.
WEB.22: Cast your burden on Yahweh, and he will sustain you. He will never allow the righteous to be moved.
WEB.23: But you, God, will bring them down into the pit of destruction. Bloodthirsty and deceitful men shall not live out half their days, but I will trust in you.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; with stringed instruments. A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem, of David. Listen to my prayer, O God,
And do not hide Yourself from my plea.
AMP.2:
Listen to me and answer me;
I am restless and distraught in my complaint and distracted
AMP.3:
Because of the voice of the enemy,
Because of the pressure of the wicked;
For they bring down trouble on me,
And in anger they persecute me.
AMP.4:
My heart is in anguish within me,
And the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
AMP.5:
Fear and trembling have come upon me;
Horror has overwhelmed me.
AMP.6:
And I say, "Oh, that I had wings like a dove!
I would fly away and be at rest.
AMP.7:
"I would wander far away,
I would lodge in the [peace of the] wilderness. Selah.
AMP.8:
"I would hurry to my refuge [my tranquil shelter far away]
From the stormy wind and from the tempest."
AMP.9:
Confuse [my enemies], O Lord, divide their tongues [destroying their schemes],
For I have seen violence and strife in the city.
AMP.10:
Day and night they go around her walls;
Wickedness and mischief are in her midst.
AMP.11:
Destruction is within her;
Oppression and deceit do not depart from her streets and market places.
AMP.12:
For it is not an enemy who taunts me—
Then I could bear it;
Nor is it one who has hated me who insolently exalts himself against me—
Then I could hide from him.
AMP.13:
But it is you, a man my equal and my counsel,
My companion and my familiar friend;
AMP.14:
We who had sweet fellowship together,
Who walked to the house of God in company.
AMP.15:
Let death come deceitfully upon them;
Let them go down alive to Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead),
For evil [of every kind] is in their dwelling and in their hearts, in their midst.
AMP.16:
As for me, I shall call upon God,
And the LORD will save me.
AMP.17:
Evening and morning and at noon I will complain and murmur,
And He will hear my voice.
AMP.18:
He has redeemed my life in peace from the battle that was against me,
For there were many against me.
AMP.19:
God will hear and humble them,
Even He who sits enthroned from old— Selah.
Because in them there has been no change [of heart],
And they do not fear God [at all].
AMP.20:
He [my companion] has put out his hands against those who were at peace with him;
He has broken his covenant [of friendship and loyalty].
AMP.21:
The words of his mouth were smoother than butter,
But his heart was hostile;
His words were softer than oil,
Yet they were drawn swords.
AMP.22:
Cast your burden on the LORD [release it] and He will sustain and uphold you;
He will never allow the righteous to be shaken (slip, fall, fail).
AMP.23:
But You, O God, will bring down the wicked to the pit of destruction;
Men of blood and treachery will not live out half their days.
But I will [boldly and unwaveringly] trust in You.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Open your ears, God, to my prayer;
don't pretend you don't hear me knocking.
MSG.2:
Come close and whisper your answer.
I really need you.
I shudder
MSG.3:
at the mean voice,
quail before the evil eye,
As they pile on the guilt,
stockpile angry slander.
MSG.4:
My insides are turned inside out;
specters of death have me down.
MSG.5:
I shake with fear,
I shudder from head to foot.
MSG.6:
"Who will give me wings," I ask—
"wings like a dove?"
Get me out of here on dove wings;
MSG.7:
I want some peace and quiet.
I want a walk in the country,
MSG.8:
I want a cabin in the woods.
I'm desperate for a change
from rage and stormy weather.
MSG.9:
Come down hard, Lord—slit their tongues.
I'm appalled how they've split the city
Into rival gangs
prowling the alleys
MSG.10:
Day and night spoiling for a fight,
trash piled in the streets,
MSG.11:
Even shopkeepers gouging and cheating
in broad daylight.
MSG.12:
This isn't the neighborhood bully
mocking me—I could take that.
This isn't a foreign devil spitting
invective—I could tune that out.
MSG.13:
It's you! We grew up together!
You! My best friend!
MSG.14:
Those long hours of leisure as we walked
arm in arm, God a third party to our conversation.
MSG.15:
Haul my betrayers off alive to hell—let them
experience the horror, let them
feel every desolate detail of a damned life.
MSG.16:
I call to God;
God will help me.
MSG.17:
At dusk, dawn, and noon I sigh
deep sighs—he hears, he rescues.
MSG.18:
My life is well and whole, secure
in the middle of danger
Even while thousands
are lined up against me.
MSG.19:
God hears it all, and from his judge's bench
puts them in their place.
But, set in their ways, they won't change;
they pay him no mind.
MSG.20:
And this, my best friend, betrayed his best friends;
his life betrayed his word.
MSG.21:
All my life I've been charmed by his speech,
never dreaming he'd turn on me.
His words, which were music to my ears,
turned to daggers in my heart.
MSG.22:
Pile your troubles on God's shoulders—
he'll carry your load, he'll help you out.
He'll never let good people
topple into ruin.
MSG.23:
But you, God, will throw the others
into a muddy bog,
Cut the lifespan of assassins
and traitors in half.
And I trust in you.

NIV.1:
Psalm 55
For the director of music. With stringed instruments. A maskil of David.
Listen to my prayer, O God,
do not ignore my plea;
NIV.2:
hear me and answer me.
My thoughts trouble me and I am distraught
NIV.3:
at the voice of the enemy,
at the stares of the wicked;
for they bring down suffering upon me
and revile me in their anger.
NIV.4:
My heart is in anguish within me;
the terrors of death assail me.
NIV.5:
Fear and trembling have beset me;
horror has overwhelmed me.
NIV.6:
I said, “Oh, that I had the wings of a dove!
I would fly away and be at rest—
NIV.7:
I would flee far away
and stay in the desert; Selah
NIV.8:
I would hurry to my place of shelter,
far from the tempest and storm.”
NIV.9:
Confuse the wicked, O Lord, confound their speech,
for I see violence and strife in the city.
NIV.10:
Day and night they prowl about on its walls;
malice and abuse are within it.
NIV.11:
Destructive forces are at work in the city;
threats and lies never leave its streets.
NIV.12:
If an enemy were insulting me,
I could endure it;
if a foe were raising himself against me,
I could hide from him.
NIV.13:
But it is you, a man like myself,
my companion, my close friend,
NIV.14:
with whom I once enjoyed sweet fellowship
as we walked with the throng at the house of God.
NIV.15:
Let death take my enemies by surprise;
let them go down alive to the grave,
for evil finds lodging among them.
NIV.16:
But I call to God,
and the Lord saves me.
NIV.17:
Evening, morning and noon
I cry out in distress,
and he hears my voice.
NIV.18:
He ransoms me unharmed
from the battle waged against me,
even though many oppose me.
NIV.19:
God, who is enthroned forever,
will hear them and afflict them— Selah
men who never change their ways
and have no fear of God.
NIV.20:
My companion attacks his friends;
he violates his covenant.
NIV.21:
His speech is smooth as butter,
yet war is in his heart;
his words are more soothing than oil,
yet they are drawn swords.
NIV.22:
Cast your cares on the Lord
and he will sustain you;
he will never let the righteous fall.
NIV.23:
But you, O God, will bring down the wicked
into the pit of corruption;
bloodthirsty and deceitful men
will not live out half their days.
But as for me, I trust in you.

NRSVCE.1:
Give ear to my prayer, O God;
do not hide yourself from my supplication.
NRSVCE.2:
Attend to me, and answer me;
I am troubled in my complaint.
I am distraught
NRSVCE.3:
by the noise of the enemy,
because of the clamor of the wicked.
For they bring trouble upon me,
and in anger they cherish enmity against me.
NRSVCE.4:
My heart is in anguish within me,
the terrors of death have fallen upon me.
NRSVCE.5:
Fear and trembling come upon me,
and horror overwhelms me.
NRSVCE.6:
And I say, “O that I had wings like a dove!
I would fly away and be at rest;
NRSVCE.7:
truly, I would flee far away;
I would lodge in the wilderness; Selah
NRSVCE.8:
I would hurry to find a shelter for myself
from the raging wind and tempest.”
NRSVCE.9:
Confuse, O Lord, confound their speech;
for I see violence and strife in the city.
NRSVCE.10:
Day and night they go around it
on its walls,
and iniquity and trouble are within it;
NRSVCE.11:
ruin is in its midst;
oppression and fraud
do not depart from its marketplace.
NRSVCE.12:
It is not enemies who taunt me —
I could bear that;
it is not adversaries who deal insolently with me —
I could hide from them.
NRSVCE.13:
But it is you, my equal,
my companion, my familiar friend,
NRSVCE.14:
with whom I kept pleasant company;
we walked in the house of God with the throng.
NRSVCE.15:
Let death come upon them;
let them go down alive to Sheol;
for evil is in their homes and in their hearts.
NRSVCE.16:
But I call upon God,
and the LORD will save me.
NRSVCE.17:
Evening and morning and at noon
I utter my complaint and moan,
and he will hear my voice.
NRSVCE.18:
He will redeem me unharmed
from the battle that I wage,
for many are arrayed against me.
NRSVCE.19:
God, who is enthroned from of old, Selah
will hear, and will humble them —
because they do not change,
and do not fear God.
NRSVCE.20:
My companion laid hands on a friend
and violated a covenant with me
NRSVCE.21:
with speech smoother than butter,
but with a heart set on war;
with words that were softer than oil,
but in fact were drawn swords.
NRSVCE.22:
Cast your burden on the LORD,
and he will sustain you;
he will never permit
the righteous to be moved.
NRSVCE.23:
But you, O God, will cast them down
into the lowest pit;
the bloodthirsty and treacherous
shall not live out half their days.
But I will trust in you.

NET.1: Listen, O God, to my prayer! Do not ignore my appeal for mercy!
NET.2: Pay attention to me and answer me! I am so upset and distressed, I am beside myself,
NET.3: because of what the enemy says, and because of how the wicked pressure me, for they hurl trouble down upon me and angrily attack me.
NET.4: My heart beats violently within me; the horrors of death overcome me.
NET.5: Fear and panic overpower me; terror overwhelms me.
NET.6: I say, “I wish I had wings like a dove! I would fly away and settle in a safe place!
NET.7: Look, I will escape to a distant place; I will stay in the wilderness. (Selah)
NET.8: I will hurry off to a place that is safe from the strong wind and the gale.”
NET.9: Confuse them, O Lord! Frustrate their plans! For I see violence and conflict in the city.
NET.10: Day and night they walk around on its walls, while wickedness and destruction are within it.
NET.11: Disaster is within it; violence and deceit do not depart from its public square.
NET.12: Indeed, it is not an enemy who insults me, or else I could bear it; it is not one who hates me who arrogantly taunts me, or else I could hide from him.
NET.13: But it is you, a man like me, my close friend in whom I confided.
NET.14: We would share personal thoughts with each other; in God’s temple we would walk together among the crowd.
NET.15: May death destroy them! May they go down alive into Sheol! For evil is in their dwelling place and in their midst.
NET.16: As for me, I will call out to God, and the LORD will deliver me.
NET.17: During the evening, morning, and noontime I will lament and moan, and he will hear me.
NET.18: He will rescue me and protect me from those who attack me, even though they greatly outnumber me.
NET.19: God, the one who has reigned as king from long ago, will hear and humiliate them. (Selah) They refuse to change, and do not fear God.
NET.20: He attacks his friends; he breaks his solemn promises to them.
NET.21: His words are as smooth as butter, but he harbors animosity in his heart. His words seem softer than oil, but they are really like sharp swords.
NET.22: Throw your burden upon the LORD, and he will sustain you. He will never allow the godly to be upended.
NET.23: But you, O God, will bring them down to the deep Pit. Violent and deceitful people will not live even half a normal lifespan. But as for me, I trust in you.
